evals.prompt: Lock GAP_001, drop GAP_002 and GAP_003. Generate the hypothesis for security-aware patch generation.
evals.expected_output: step8_hypothesis_specification.md with 3-4 RQs about security-aware APR, formal H0/H1 hypotheses with quantified expectations derived from SOTA (e.g., vulnerability rate reduction), IN/OUT scope covering LLM-based repair + security validation but excluding non-LLM repair approaches, and step8_journal_recommendations.md with SE/security journals. Bilingual throughout.
expectations: step8_hypothesis_specification.md exists and contains YAML frontmatter with selected_gap: GAP_001
step8_journal_recommendations.md exists and recommends at least 3 journals
At least 3 research questions (RQ1, RQ2, RQ3) are defined
Primary hypothesis H1 includes a directional, testable statement about security-aware patch generation
Null hypothesis H0 is explicitly stated as the logical negation of H1
IN scope table covers at minimum: Population, Intervention/Method, Comparison, Primary Outcome, Design
OUT scope table has at least 2 exclusions, each with an academically defensible rationale
Hypotheses reference specific quantitative metrics or benchmarks from the SOTA review (e.g., vulnerability rates, Defects4J counts)
A suggested statistical approach is named for the primary hypothesis
Content is bilingual — both English and Traditional Chinese appear in substantive sections
Gap-to-Hypothesis traceability table exists linking RQs and hypotheses back to GAP_001 evidence
Journal recommendations include impact factors and scope fit explanations

evals.prompt: 我想鎖定 GAP_001，就是那個關於 biomarker-personalized NF 缺乏假回饋對照 RCT 的缺口。幫我生成假說。
evals.expected_output: step8_hypothesis_specification.md with RQs about sham-controlled RCT for biomarker-personalized neurofeedback in ADHD children, H0/H1 about NF specificity when personalized by EEG phenotype, IN/OUT scope covering specific populations and NF protocol types, journal recommendations targeting clinical neuroscience / ADHD journals. Bilingual. Should handle Chinese user input naturally.
expectations: step8_hypothesis_specification.md exists and contains YAML frontmatter with selected_gap: GAP_001
step8_journal_recommendations.md exists and recommends at least 3 journals
At least 3 research questions (RQ1, RQ2, RQ3) are defined
Primary hypothesis H1 addresses sham-controlled RCT for biomarker-personalized neurofeedback
Null hypothesis H0 is explicitly stated
IN scope specifies a pediatric/child ADHD population consistent with the session topic
OUT scope table has at least 2 exclusions with rationale
Content is bilingual — both English and Traditional Chinese appear in substantive sections (RQs, hypotheses, scope)
The hypothesis references specific papers or effect sizes from the SOTA review (citation keys used)
A suggested statistical approach is named for the primary hypothesis
The skill correctly identified GAP_001 from the Chinese user input
Journal recommendations target clinical neuroscience, ADHD, or neurofeedback-relevant journals

evals.prompt: I want to go with the long-term durability gap — GAP_001. But I should mention we can only do a 12-month observational follow-up study, no new RCT. Please generate hypothesis accordingly.
evals.expected_output: step8_hypothesis_specification.md that respects the user's methodological constraint (observational follow-up, not RCT). RQs should be framed around durability assessment rather than treatment efficacy. H1 should predict maintenance of effects at 6/12 months. IN scope should specify observational design; OUT scope should explicitly exclude new treatment administration. Journal recommendations should target journals receptive to follow-up/observational studies. Bilingual.
expectations: step8_hypothesis_specification.md exists and contains YAML frontmatter with selected_gap: GAP_001
step8_journal_recommendations.md exists and recommends at least 3 journals
At least 3 research questions are defined
Study design in IN scope is observational/follow-up, NOT RCT — respecting user constraint
OUT scope explicitly excludes new treatment administration or RCT design, with rationale
Timeframe in IN scope references 12 months or 12-month follow-up
Primary hypothesis H1 is about durability/maintenance of effects, not treatment efficacy
Null hypothesis H0 is explicitly stated
Content is bilingual — both English and Traditional Chinese appear in substantive sections
The hypothesis references specific papers or effect sizes from the SOTA review
A suggested statistical approach is named (appropriate for longitudinal/observational data)
Journal recommendations include journals receptive to observational or follow-up studies
